findings.title: Missing Owner Check
findings.description: The legacy_position account is a raw AccountInfo without any owner or discriminator checks, allowing any account with compatible bytes to be accepted, leading to potential spoofing.
findings.impact: Attacker-controlled accounts can spoof valid legacy_position data and claim rewards proportional to a fake staked amount.
findings.recommendation: Replace with `Account<'info, LegacyPosition>` (Anchor enforces owner + discriminator), or add an explicit require_keys_eq!(legacy_position.owner, &crate::ID).

findings.title: Account Data Matching
findings.description: Manual deserialization of legacy_position data bypasses Anchor's discriminator and owner checks, allowing attacker-controlled accounts to spoof valid data.
findings.impact: Two-step account spoof combined with the missing owner check feeds attacker bytes into reward calculations.
findings.recommendation: Avoid manual deserialization on raw AccountInfo; instead, use Anchor Account types with proper ownership and discriminator enforcement.

findings.title: Integer Overflow
findings.description: In apply_rewards, the multiplication ctx.accounts.pool.reward_rate * multiplier is unchecked and can overflow silently, leading to incorrect reward rates.
findings.impact: Today bounded by an upstream u8 cap (≤10); a future refactor widening multiplier would silently enable overflow.
findings.recommendation: Use checked_mul(... as u64).ok_or(ErrorCode::Overflow)?, and enable overflow-checks = true in the release profile.
